_meta.purpose: Secondary cross-check layer — validates or warns against structural analysis output
_meta.usage: Never use as primary driver. Use AFTER Stage 3 structural analysis to confirm or raise flags.
_meta.confidenceImpact: Match = +1 confidence. Mismatch = warning flag, not override.

warns: Subsequence DP — contiguous means no skipping
exactPhrases.note: Contiguous = no gaps. Subsequence DP is wrong here unless problem also asks for subsequence.
If sum equals target — Prefix Sum + HashMap. If max sum — Kadane.
Substring = contiguous characters. Sliding window is the first thing to try.

warns: Sliding Window — subsequence allows gaps, window does not
exactPhrases.note: Subsequence = can skip elements. Almost always DP. LIS specifically has O(n log n) solution.

signals: Binary Search + patience sorting O(n log n)
exactPhrases.note: Classic LIS. At n=10^5 must use O(n log n) binary search approach.

warns: DFS — DFS does not find shortest path in general graphs
exactPhrases.note: First question: weighted or not? Second: negative weights? Third: single source or all pairs?

warns: Dijkstra — overkill for unweighted, use BFS
exactPhrases.note: Steps = edges = unweighted. BFS gives shortest path in O(V+E).
Implies DAG. If cycle detection needed alongside — use DFS 3-color.
For directed graphs use DFS coloring. For undirected use Union Find. For linked list use slow/fast pointer.

warns: Brute Force O(n^2) — always has O(n) stack solution
exactPhrases.note: Classic monotonic stack. Maintain decreasing stack, pop when current is greater.
Mirror of next greater. Maintain increasing stack.

warns: Segment Tree — overkill, deque is O(n)
exactPhrases.note: Sliding window max/min in O(n) using monotonic deque. Classic interview problem.
0/1 = each item used once. Unbounded = items reusable. Check which variant explicitly.
If all combinations needed — Backtracking. If just count — DP counting variant.

warns: Pure DP without bitmask — cannot represent permutation state in polynomial space for general n
exactPhrases.note: All permutations = Backtracking. Optimal permutation (TSP-type) = Bitmask DP if n<=20.
Partition into equal subsets — DP. Partition into k groups to minimize max — Binary Search on Answer.
Classic interval DP. dp[i][j] = min cost to multiply matrices i through j.
Longest palindromic substring — Manacher's O(n). Count palindromes — expand around center O(n^2) or Manacher.
Fixed window size = frequency comparison. Variable window = sliding window with counts.
If values bounded (0-26 chars) — direct array. If unbounded — HashMap.
Merge intervals — sort by start, greedy merge. Count overlaps — line sweep or sort.
Minimum rooms = max overlapping at any point. Use events or priority queue.

warns: Direct DP — this is almost always binary search on answer
exactPhrases.note: Monotonic property: if minimum X is achievable, minimum X-1 is also achievable. Binary search on X.
Mirror of maximize minimum. Binary search on the answer value.

warns: Full sort — O(n log n) when O(n) or O(n log k) exists
exactPhrases.note: Min-heap of size k = O(n log k). QuickSelect = O(n) average. Binary Search on value = O(n log(max_val)).
Mirror of kth largest.
Static graph — DFS/BFS. Dynamic (edges added) — Union Find. Directed — Tarjan/Kosaraju for SCC.
Kruskal better for sparse. Prim better for dense. Both O(E log E).
Both O(V+E). Tarjan is one pass. Kosaraju is two DFS passes.
Check bipartiteness — BFS coloring. Bipartite matching — Hungarian or Hopcroft-Karp.

warns: DFS for large grids — stack overflow risk at n=10^6. Use BFS.
exactPhrases.note: Grid connectivity. BFS is safer for large grids due to stack depth.

warns: Backtracking for large n — will TLE
exactPhrases.note: Count all valid configurations — DP. If modular arithmetic — add mod to DP transitions.

warns: Standard DP — cannot easily produce lex smallest
exactPhrases.note: Always build greedily from left — at each position choose the smallest valid option.
Tracking distinct elements — HashSet O(1). In sorted array — two pointer.
Static vs dynamic is the key split. Static — Prefix Sum or Sparse Table. Dynamic — Segment Tree or Fenwick.

warns: Fenwick Tree basic — does not support range updates without modification
exactPhrases.note: If updates and queries both — Lazy Segment Tree. If only updates then one final read — Difference Array.

signals: Two Pointer on sorted array O(n)
exactPhrases.note: Unsorted — HashMap. Sorted — Two Pointer. Three sum — fix one + two pointer.
Classic DP with states: holding / not holding / cooldown. Transaction limit drives state count.

warns: Greedy — fails for arbitrary coin denominations
exactPhrases.note: Minimum coins = DP. Count ways = DP counting. Greedy only works for canonical coin systems.
dp[i] = can string[0..i] be segmented. Use Trie for efficient dictionary lookup.
Classic 2D DP. dp[i][j] = min edits to convert s1[0..i] to s2[0..j].
Grid path with uniform step cost — 2D DP. Variable costs — Dijkstra.
Can you reach end — Greedy O(n). Minimum jumps — Greedy or BFS O(n).
Tree serialization — preorder DFS with null markers. Deserialize — reconstruct from same order.

misdirectionWarnings.surface: Looks like a simple greedy interval problem
misdirectionWarnings.checkQuestion: Do intervals have weights/values? If yes — greedy by earliest end time FAILS. Use DP.
Are all edge weights equal? If no — BFS gives wrong answer. Use Dijkstra or 0-1 BFS.
Can a locally optimal choice lead to globally suboptimal result? Try a small counter-example.

misdirectionWarnings.surface: Contiguous subarray — looks like sliding window
misdirectionWarnings.checkQuestion: Is the window validity condition monotonic? If not — sliding window breaks. Use DP or prefix sum.
Is there an ordering constraint? If yes — model as DAG and use topological DP.

misdirectionWarnings.surface: Find a value — looks like binary search on array
misdirectionWarnings.checkQuestion: Are you searching for a value in an array OR searching for an answer value that satisfies a condition? These are different.
Is the graph guaranteed to be a tree (connected, n-1 edges, no cycles)? If not — cannot use tree algorithms.

contextualSignals.implication: Count of solutions is exponentially large. Must be DP counting variant.
contextualSignals.note: Whenever you see mod 10^9+7 — the answer is a count, not a direct value.

contextualSignals.signal: values in range 0 to 26 (or 0 to 9, or 0 to 1)
contextualSignals.implication: Direct array indexing instead of HashMap. Frequency arrays over HashMaps.
contextualSignals.note: Small bounded values — use array not map. 26 for lowercase letters, 10 for digits.

contextualSignals.signal: n is large but k is small (k <= 20)
contextualSignals.implication: Bitmask DP over k items is feasible. DP state can include subset of k.
contextualSignals.note: Two-level structure — outer loop over n, inner bitmask over k.

contextualSignals.signal: queries arrive one by one, must answer before next
contextualSignals.implication: Cannot use offline algorithms. Eliminates Mo's Algorithm.
contextualSignals.note: Online constraint — use Segment Tree or Fenwick, not Mo's.

contextualSignals.signal: T test cases at start of input
contextualSignals.implication: Preprocessing outside test case loop. Per-case complexity must be low.
contextualSignals.note: Total complexity = T * per_case_complexity. Precompute primes, factorials, etc. outside.

contextualSignals.implication: DFS from root. dp[node] defined in terms of dp[children]. Post-order traversal.
contextualSignals.note: Standard tree DP pattern. Process children before parent.

contextualSignals.signal: print the actual path / sequence / solution
contextualSignals.implication: Must store parent pointers or backtracking info. Cannot just store optimal value.
contextualSignals.note: Reconstruction requires storing prev[] or parent[] array alongside dp[] array.

contextualSignals.implication: Bitset operations possible. XOR / AND / OR tricks applicable.
contextualSignals.note: Binary constraints open up bit manipulation approaches.

contextualSignals.implication: BFS for shortest path. DFS for reachability. DP if movement is one-directional.
contextualSignals.note: If movement is only right and down — DP. If all directions — BFS/DFS.

contextualSignals.implication: Single pattern — KMP or Z-algorithm. Multiple patterns — Aho-Corasick.
contextualSignals.note: Never use naive O(n*m) matching in competitive programming. Always KMP minimum.
